pattern: Sliding window on the complement: maximize the sum of k cards taken from both ends by minimizing the sum of the middle subarray of length n-k.
hints: Taking k cards from the two ends leaves a contiguous window of n-k cards in the middle untouched.
Minimize the sum of that middle window as it slides from left to right; the answer is total_sum minus that minimum window sum.
Use a fixed-size sliding window of length n-k, updating the running sum as it shifts one position at a time.

edge_inputs.description: take one card, best is max of first or last
equal values — any k cards give same sum
optimal requires mixing from both ends
all high on left, low in middle, high on right
k equals length-1, one card left in middle

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 1000))
    cards = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [cards, k]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maxScore(self, cardPoints, k):
        n = len(cardPoints)
        total = sum(cardPoints)
        if k == n:
            return total
        # Minimize middle window of size n-k
        w = n - k
        win_sum = sum(cardPoints[:w])
        min_win = win_sum
        for i in range(w, n):
            win_sum += cardPoints[i] - cardPoints[i - w]
            min_win = min(min_win, win_sum)
        return total - min_win
